title: Genome-Edited Rice
focus: India's first genome-edited rice varieties developed by ICAR and their characteristics.
rationale: Highly examinable due to the distinction between GE and GM crops, and India being the first country to develop these specific varieties.
sectionExcerpt: ICAR developed India's first genome-edited (GE) rice varieties: DRR Rice 100 (Kamla) and Pusa DST Rice, making India the first country to do so. Developed using CRISPR-Cas. DRR Dhan 100 Kamala targets the CKX2 gene. Pusa DST Rice 1 improves MTU1010. They contain no foreign DNA and are not considered GM Crops.
evidenceBullets: ICAR developed India's first GE rice varieties: DRR Rice 100 (Kamla) and Pusa DST Rice.
India is the first country in the world to develop GE rice varieties.
DRR Dhan 100 Kamala targets the CKX2 (Cytokinin Oxidase 2) gene.
These varieties contain no foreign DNA and are therefore not considered GM Crops.

title: Genome India Project and Sequencing Initiatives
focus: The GenomeIndia Project by DBT, data storage at IBDC, and other national/international genome sequencing initiatives.
rationale: Important national scientific initiative mapping genetic diversity, with clear institutional anchors (DBT, IBDC, CSIR) suitable for match-the-following or statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Initiated by the Department of Biotechnology (DBT) to map India's genetic diversity. Data is stored at the Indian Biological Data Centre (IBDC) at Faridabad, India's first national life science data repository. Other initiatives include IndiGen (CSIR), One Day One Genome (DBT), Human Genome Project, 100,000 Genomes Project (England), and International HapMap Project.
evidenceBullets: GenomeIndia Project was initiated by the Department of Biotechnology (DBT) to map India's genetic diversity.
Data is stored at the Indian Biological Data Centre (IBDC) at Faridabad, India's first national life science data repository.
IndiGen program was initiated by CSIR.
One Day One Genome Initiative was initiated by DBT.
International HapMap Project analyzes variants in African, Asian, and European ancestry groups.

title: Gene-Drive Technology (GDT)
focus: Mechanism and applications of Gene-Drive Technology in altering inheritance patterns.
rationale: Conceptual topic in biotechnology with direct applications in public health (vector-borne diseases) and agriculture (pests).
sectionExcerpt: Gene-Drive Technology (GDT) can stop insects from spreading vector-borne diseases and pests. It is a genetic engineering technique that modifies genes to alter normal Mendelian inheritance, increasing the likelihood that a particular suite of genes will be passed onto the next generation.
evidenceBullets: GDT can stop insects from spreading vector-borne diseases like mosquitoes and agricultural pests.
It is a genetic engineering technique that alters normal Mendelian inheritance.
GDT increases the likelihood that a specific suite of genes is passed onto the next generation.
